title: plantago paralias var. pumila
intro: Plantago paralias var. pumila, also known as dwarf plantain or small plantain, is a small, annual or biennial plant belonging to the Plantaginaceae family, characterized by its oval-shaped leaves and small, brown or black seeds. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Plantago paralias var. pumila include its potential to treat digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and its leaves and seeds can also be used in teas, salves, and poultices to harness its medicinal properties.
